page: Amazon Web Services c7gn.8xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by the ARMv8 64-bit architecture. It features 32 dedicated physical cores utilizing the AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 processor running at 2.6 GHz, with one thread per core. The instance is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying entirely on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data shows strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score ranking in the top 10% of tested instances. Large-block memory bandwidth is also strong, reaching 248.85 GB/sec for read-only operations and 106.15 GB/sec for write operations. Multi-core performance is robust, as evidenced by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17673.0 and a Passmark database operations score of 31190.05, both placing in the top 25%. However, single-core metrics and large-model LLM inference prompt processing fall into the average and weak tiers respectively, indicating a tradeoff where multi-threaded and memory-bound workloads perform significantly better than single-threaded or heavy AI inference tasks.

With a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps, this instance is designed for network-intensive and compute-bound workloads. The high network throughput combined with strong database and web serving benchmarks makes it suitable for distributed databases, network appliances, containerized microservices, and multi-threaded data processing. The ARM64 architecture offers a cost-efficient alternative to x86-64 instances for workloads optimized for Graviton processors, though compatibility with legacy x86 software remains a key deployment consideration.
description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 physical cores running at 2.6 GHz. It features 64.0 GB of RAM, providing a 2.0 GB per core ratio, and operates on the Nitro hypervisor without local storage or GPU accelerators. The instance delivers 100 Gbps of baseline network bandwidth. In benchmark testing, it demonstrates top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and large-block read memory bandwidth reaching 248.85 GB/sec. Multi-core capabilities are strong, highlighted by a Geekbench multi-core score of 17673.0 and high database operation throughput. However, single-core performance is average, and large-model LLM prompt processing is weak. This instance is suited for network-intensive applications, multi-threaded data processing, and distributed database workloads optimized for ARM64 architecture.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.8xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 32 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7gn.8xlarge features 32 ARM64 Graviton cores, 64 GB RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth for compute-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 compute-optimized instance delivering 100 Gbps network bandwidth and top-tier memory throughput for multi-threaded and network-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 32 dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V1 ARM64 cores at 2.6 GHz.
Equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Top-tier memory performance with large-block read bandwidth of 248.85 GB/sec.
Strong multi-core execution, scoring 17673.0 on Geekbench multi-core benchmarks.
No local storage or GPU hardware, requiring network-attached storage.
Suited for distributed databases, network appliances, and ARM-optimized microservices.
